task: Classify a customer-support message into one of 10 intent labels: refund, cancel, billing, shipping, password_reset, account_lock, complaint, feedback, escalate, other. Designed as the Predibase/LoRAX-style 1000-row demo — the rule-class compiled artifact should match the LLM on intent recall while costing $0/call and running in microseconds.

recipes.name: customer-support intent classifier (keyword router)

input.text: I would like a refund for order #1001
process a refund please, charge was duplicate
I want my money back for invoice 7392
how do I cancel my subscription?
stop my plan I no longer need it
turn off my subscription, stop billing me
I forgot my password
send me a password reset link
two-factor not working, locked out
my account is locked
account temporarily disabled, please help
security lock on my account, need to verify
I need to speak with a supervisor immediately
escalate this to your manager please
tier 2 support please, this is beyond tier 1
where is my order #1001?
tracking number for shipment 7392 please
shipment delayed, no updates in days
why was I charged $29.00 on 2025-04-08?
update my payment method please
send me a receipt for the 2025-05-12 payment
great service, just wanted to say thanks
love the new feature, keep it up
one feature request: bulk export to CSV
this is the worst service I have ever used
support agent was rude and unhelpful
I have been waiting for 5 days with no response
do you have any openings on your team?
when is your office open?
is there a partner program?

training_stats.approach: deterministic keyword + regex rules over 10 ordered classes; first match wins
